```text
     1→/**
     2→ * APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker
     3→ *
     4→ * Serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a stable URL.
     5→ * Uses Cloudflare's edge cache for both the API response and the
     6→ * image bytes — no KV, no cron, no external storage.
     7→ *
     8→ * Routes:
     9→ *   GET /      → today's APOD image (high res, falls back to standard)
    10→ *   GET /sd    → today's APOD image (standard res)
    11→ *   GET /info  → JSON metadata
    12→ *   GET /about → plain-text description and attribution
    13→ */
    14→
    15→export interface Env {
    16→  NASA_API_KEY?: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→const NASA_APOD_URL = "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod";
    20→const MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS = 7;
    21→const CACHE_TTL = 3600;
    22→
    23→interface ApodResponse {
    24→  date: string;
    25→  title: string;
    26→  explanation: string;
    27→  url: string;
    28→  hdurl?: string;
    29→  media_type: "image" | "video";
    30→  copyright?: string;
    31→}
    32→
    33→function formatDate(d: Date): string {
    34→  return d.toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    35→}
    36→
    37→class NasaApiError extends Error {
    38→  constructor(public statusCode: number, message: string) {
    39→    super(message);
    40→  }
    41→}
    42→
    43→async function fetchApod(date: string, apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    44→  const url = `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${date}`;
    45→
    46→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    47→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    48→  });
    49→
    50→  if (!res.ok) {
    51→    throw new NasaApiError(res.status,
    52→      res.status === 429
    53→        ? "NASA API rate limit exceeded — try again later"
    54→        : `NASA API returned ${res.status}`
    55→    );
    56→  }
    57→
    58→  return res.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
    59→}
    60→
    61→let cachedImage: { date: string; apod: ApodResponse } | null = null;
    62→
    63→async function findLatestImage(apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    64→  const today = formatDate(new Date());
    65→
    66→  if (cachedImage && cachedImage.date === today) {
    67→    return cachedImage.apod;
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  let videoStreak = 0;
    71→
    72→  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
    73→    const d = new Date();
    74→    d.setUTCDate(d.getUTCDate() - i);
    75→
    76→    const apod = await fetchApod(formatDate(d), apiKey);
    77→    if (apod.media_type === "image") {
    78→      cachedImage = { date: today, apod };
    79→      return apod;
    80→    }
    81→    videoStreak++;
    82→  }
    83→
    84→  throw new Error(
    85→    `Today's APOD is a video, and the last ${videoStreak} days have been too — no image available`
    86→  );
    87→}
    88→
    89→async function fetchImageCached(url: string): Promise<Response> {
    90→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    91→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    92→  });
    93→
    94→  if (!res.ok) {
    95→    throw new Error(`Image fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  return res;
    99→}
   100→
   101→function baseHeaders(origin: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
   102→  return {
   103→    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   104→    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
   105→    "Link": [
   106→      `<${origin}/>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="HD image"`,
   107→      `<${origin}/sd>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="SD image"`,
   108→      `<${origin}/info>; rel="describedby"`,
   109→      `<${origin}/about>; rel="about"`,
   110→    ].join(", "),
   111→    ...headers,
   112→  };
   113→}
   114→
   115→export default {
   116→  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
   117→    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
   118→      return new Response(null, {
   119→        headers: {
   120→          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   121→          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, OPTIONS",
   122→          "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
   123→        },
   124→      });
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    const reqUrl = new URL(request.url);
   128→    const { pathname } = reqUrl;
   129→    const origin = reqUrl.origin;
   130→
   131→    try {
   132→      const apiKey = env.NASA_API_KEY || "DEMO_KEY";
   133→
   134→      if (pathname === "/" || pathname === "/sd") {
   135→        const apod = await findLatestImage(apiKey);
   136→        const imageUrl = pathname === "/sd"
   137→          ? apod.url
   138→          : (apod.hdurl || apod.url);
   139→
   140→        const upstream = await fetchImageCached(imageUrl);
   141→        const contentType = upstream.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/jpeg";
   142→
   143→        return new Response(upstream.body, {
   144→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),
   145→        });
   146→      }
   147→
   148→      if (pathname === "/info") {
   149→        const endpoints = {
   150→          "/": "Today's APOD image (high resolution, falls back to standard)",
   151→          "/sd": "Today's APOD image (standard resolution)",
   152→          "/info": "JSON metadata for today's APOD",
   153→          "/about": "Plain-text description and attribution",
   154→        };
   155→
   156→        let apodData = {};
   157→        try {
   158→          const apod = await fetchApod(formatDate(new Date()), apiKey);
   159→          apodData = {
   160→            title: apod.title,
   161→            date: apod.date,
   162→            explanation: apod.explanation,
   163→            media_type: apod.media_type,
   164→            url: apod.url,
   165→            hdurl: apod.hdurl ?? null,
   166→            copyright: apod.copyright ?? null,
   167→          };
   168→        } catch {
   169→          apodData = { apod_error: "Unable to fetch today's APOD — NASA API may be unavailable" };
   170→        }
   171→
   172→        return new Response(
   173→          JSON.stringify({ ...apodData, endpoints }, null, 2),
   174→          { headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   175→        );
   176→      }
   177→
   178→      if (pathname === "/about") {
   179→        const text = [
   180→          "",
   181→          "     .        *          .          *          .        *",
   182→          "   .         *                        .          .",
   183→          "                 _   _    _    ____    _",
   184→          "     *          | \\ | |  / \\  / ___|  / \\         .",
   185→          "        .       |  \\| | / _ \\ \\___ \\ / _ \\",
   186→          "           .    | |\\  |/ ___ \\ ___) / ___ \\   *",
   187→          "     .          |_| \\_/_/   \\_\\____/_/   \\_\\        .",
   188→          "  *                              .                *",
   189→          "        .    *        .    *          .        .",
   190→          "",
   191→          "                   ── APOD Proxy ──",
   192→          "",
   193→          "  Proxy for NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day.",
   194→          "  Returns today's image at a static URL, no API key required.",
   195→          "  Video days are skipped automatically.",
   196→          "  Useful for wallpapers, <img> sources, and other zero-JS environments.",
   197→          "",
   198→          "  Usage:",
   199→          `    <img src="${origin}/">`,
   200→          "",
   201→          "  Routes:",
   202→          "    /        HD image (default)",
   203→          "    /sd      Standard resolution",
   204→          "    /info    JSON metadata",
   205→          "    /about   This page",
   206→          "",
   207→          "  Built by Grant Keller — https://grantkeller.dev",
   208→          "",
   209→        ].join("\n");
   210→
   211→        return new Response(text, {
   212→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" }),
   213→        });
   214→      }
   215→
   216→      return new Response(
   217→        JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found. Routes: /, /sd, /info, /about" }),
   218→        { status: 404, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   219→      );
   220→    } catch (err) {
   221→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   222→      const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   223→      return new Response(
   224→        JSON.stringify({ error: message }),
   225→        { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   226→      );
   227→    }
   228→  },
   229→};
   230→
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 07:28:07Z)**_

The `/info` endpoint catches errors internally (line 168) and returns a soft `apod_error` key, while `/` and `/sd` let errors propagate to the outer catch (line 220) which returns a proper error response with status code. I'll make `/info` match the other endpoints by letting errors propagate.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./src/index.ts`

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker
     3→ *
     4→ * Serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a stable URL.
     5→ * Uses Cloudflare's edge cache for both the API response and the
     6→ * image bytes — no KV, no cron, no external storage.
     7→ *
     8→ * Routes:
     9→ *   GET /      → today's APOD image (high res, falls back to standard)
    10→ *   GET /sd    → today's APOD image (standard res)
    11→ *   GET /info  → JSON metadata
    12→ *   GET /about → plain-text description and attribution
    13→ */
    14→
    15→export interface Env {
    16→  NASA_API_KEY?: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→const NASA_APOD_URL = "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod";
    20→const MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS = 7;
    21→const CACHE_TTL = 3600;
    22→
    23→interface ApodResponse {
    24→  date: string;
    25→  title: string;
    26→  explanation: string;
    27→  url: string;
    28→  hdurl?: string;
    29→  media_type: "image" | "video";
    30→  copyright?: string;
    31→}
    32→
    33→function formatDate(d: Date): string {
    34→  return d.toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    35→}
    36→
    37→class NasaApiError extends Error {
    38→  constructor(public statusCode: number, message: string) {
    39→    super(message);
    40→  }
    41→}
    42→
    43→async function fetchApod(date: string, apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    44→  const url = `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${date}`;
    45→
    46→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    47→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    48→  });
    49→
    50→  if (!res.ok) {
    51→    throw new NasaApiError(res.status,
    52→      res.status === 429
    53→        ? "NASA API rate limit exceeded — try again later"
    54→        : `NASA API returned ${res.status}`
    55→    );
    56→  }
    57→
    58→  return res.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
    59→}
    60→
    61→let cachedImage: { date: string; apod: ApodResponse } | null = null;
    62→
    63→async function findLatestImage(apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    64→  const today = formatDate(new Date());
    65→
    66→  if (cachedImage && cachedImage.date === today) {
    67→    return cachedImage.apod;
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  let videoStreak = 0;
    71→
    72→  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
    73→    const d = new Date();
    74→    d.setUTCDate(d.getUTCDate() - i);
    75→
    76→    const apod = await fetchApod(formatDate(d), apiKey);
    77→    if (apod.media_type === "image") {
    78→      cachedImage = { date: today, apod };
    79→      return apod;
    80→    }
    81→    videoStreak++;
    82→  }
    83→
    84→  throw new Error(
    85→    `Today's APOD is a video, and the last ${videoStreak} days have been too — no image available`
    86→  );
    87→}
    88→
    89→async function fetchImageCached(url: string): Promise<Response> {
    90→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    91→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    92→  });
    93→
    94→  if (!res.ok) {
    95→    throw new Error(`Image fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  return res;
    99→}
   100→
   101→function baseHeaders(origin: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
   102→  return {
   103→    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   104→    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
   105→    "Link": [
   106→      `<${origin}/>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="HD image"`,
   107→      `<${origin}/sd>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="SD image"`,
   108→      `<${origin}/info>; rel="describedby"`,
   109→      `<${origin}/about>; rel="about"`,
   110→    ].join(", "),
   111→    ...headers,
   112→  };
   113→}
   114→
   115→export default {
   116→  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
   117→    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
   118→      return new Response(null, {
   119→        headers: {
   120→          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   121→          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, OPTIONS",
   122→          "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
   123→        },
   124→      });
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    const reqUrl = new URL(request.url);
   128→    const { pathname } = reqUrl;
   129→    const origin = reqUrl.origin;
   130→
   131→    try {
   132→      const apiKey = env.NASA_API_KEY || "DEMO_KEY";
   133→
   134→      if (pathname === "/" || pathname === "/sd") {
   135→        const apod = await findLatestImage(apiKey);
   136→        const imageUrl = pathname === "/sd"
   137→          ? apod.url
   138→          : (apod.hdurl || apod.url);
   139→
   140→        const upstream = await fetchImageCached(imageUrl);
   141→        const contentType = upstream.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/jpeg";
   142→
   143→        return new Response(upstream.body, {
   144→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),
   145→        });
   146→      }
   147→
   148→      if (pathname === "/info") {
   149→        const endpoints = {
   150→          "/": "Today's APOD image (high resolution, falls back to standard)",
   151→          "/sd": "Today's APOD image (standard resolution)",
   152→          "/info": "JSON metadata for today's APOD",
   153→          "/about": "Plain-text description and attribution",
   154→        };
   155→
   156→        try {
   157→          const apod = await fetchApod(formatDate(new Date()), apiKey);
   158→
   159→          return new Response(
   160→            JSON.stringify({
   161→              title: apod.title,
   162→              date: apod.date,
   163→              explanation: apod.explanation,
   164→              media_type: apod.media_type,
   165→              url: apod.url,
   166→              hdurl: apod.hdurl ?? null,
   167→              copyright: apod.copyright ?? null,
   168→              endpoints,
   169→            }, null, 2),
   170→            { headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   171→          );
   172→        } catch (err) {
   173→          const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   174→          const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   175→          return new Response(
   176→            JSON.stringify({ error: message, endpoints }, null, 2),
   177→            { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   178→          );
   179→        }
   180→      }
   181→
   182→      if (pathname === "/about") {
   183→        const text = [
   184→          "",
   185→          "     .        *          .          *          .        *",
   186→          "   .         *                        .          .",
   187→          "                 _   _    _    ____    _",
   188→          "     *          | \\ | |  / \\  / ___|  / \\         .",
   189→          "        .       |  \\| | / _ \\ \\___ \\ / _ \\",
   190→          "           .    | |\\  |/ ___ \\ ___) / ___ \\   *",
   191→          "     .          |_| \\_/_/   \\_\\____/_/   \\_\\        .",

   194→          "",

   196→          "",
   197→          "  Proxy for NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day.",
   198→          "  Returns today's image at a static URL, no API key required.",
   199→          "  Video days are skipped automatically.",
   200→          "  Useful for wallpapers, <img> sources, and other zero-JS environments.",

   204→          "",

   206→          "    /        HD image (default)",
   207→          "    /sd      Standard resolution",
   208→          "    /info    JSON metadata",
   209→          "    /about   This page",
   210→          "",
   211→          "  Built by Grant Keller — https://grantkeller.dev",
   212→          "",
   213→        ].join("\n");
   214→
   215→        return new Response(text, {
   216→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" }),
   217→        });
   218→      }
   219→
   220→      return new Response(
   221→        JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found. Routes: /, /sd, /info, /about" }),
   222→        { status: 404, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   223→      );
   224→    } catch (err) {
   225→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   226→      const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   227→      return new Response(
   228→        JSON.stringify({ error: message }),
   229→        { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   230→      );
   231→    }
   232→  },

```text
     1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## Project Overview
     6→
     7→APOD Proxy — a Cloudflare Worker that proxies NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day API behind stable, fixed URLs. Serves today's APOD image (standard or HD) or JSON metadata. Falls back up to 7 days when the daily APOD is a video.
     8→
     9→## Commands
    10→
    11→```bash
    12→npm run dev        # Local dev server at http://localhost:8787
    13→npm run deploy     # Deploy to Cloudflare Workers
    14→npx tsc --noEmit   # Type-check (no build artifact — Wrangler bundles directly)
    15→```
    16→
    17→## Architecture
    18→
    19→Single-file Worker (`src/index.ts`) with four routes:
    20→
    21→- `GET /` — proxies today's HD APOD image (falls back to standard if unavailable)
    22→- `GET /sd` — proxies the standard-res version
    23→- `GET /info` — returns JSON metadata
    24→- `GET /about` — plain-text description and attribution
    25→
    26→Caching is handled entirely by Cloudflare's edge cache via `cf: { cacheTtl, cacheEverything }` on fetch calls — no KV, D1, or cron. Cache key changes naturally when the date rolls over (new date string = new API URL).
    27→
    28→The `findLatestImage` function walks backwards from today up to `MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS` (7) to skip video APODs.
    29→
    30→## Environment
    31→
    32→- **`NASA_API_KEY`** — optional secret; falls back to `DEMO_KEY` (30 req/hr, sufficient behind cache). Set via `npx wrangler secret put NASA_API_KEY`.
    33→- Local secrets go in `.dev.vars` (gitignored).
    34→
    35→## Project Board
    36→
    37→This project uses a structured board system for project management.
    38→
    39→- **Ask before creating new board files** — don't auto-create tasks, ADRs, etc.
    40→- Follow the board system spec for all documentation artifacts
    41→- Use `/board:board-show` to see current board state
    42→- Use `/board:board-add` to create new items
    43→- Use `/board:board-update` after completing significant work to reconcile the board
    44→- Use `/board:board-check` when starting work to find related board context
    45→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker
     3→ *
     4→ * Serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a stable URL.
     5→ * Uses Cloudflare's edge cache for both the API response and the
     6→ * image bytes — no KV, no cron, no external storage.
     7→ *
     8→ * Routes:
     9→ *   GET /      → today's APOD image (high res, falls back to standard)
    10→ *   GET /sd    → today's APOD image (standard res)
    11→ *   GET /info  → JSON metadata
    12→ *   GET /about → plain-text description and attribution
    13→ */
    14→
    15→export interface Env {
    16→  NASA_API_KEY?: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→const NASA_APOD_URL = "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod";
    20→const MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS = 7;
    21→const CACHE_TTL = 3600;
    22→
    23→interface ApodResponse {
    24→  date: string;
    25→  title: string;
    26→  explanation: string;
    27→  url: string;
    28→  hdurl?: string;
    29→  media_type: "image" | "video";
    30→  copyright?: string;
    31→}
    32→
    33→function formatDate(d: Date): string {
    34→  return d.toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    35→}
    36→
    37→class NasaApiError extends Error {
    38→  constructor(public statusCode: number, message: string) {
    39→    super(message);
    40→  }
    41→}
    42→
    43→async function fetchApod(date: string, apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    44→  const url = `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${date}`;
    45→
    46→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    47→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    48→  });
    49→
    50→  if (!res.ok) {
    51→    throw new NasaApiError(res.status,
    52→      res.status === 429
    53→        ? "NASA API rate limit exceeded — try again later"
    54→        : `NASA API returned ${res.status}`
    55→    );
    56→  }
    57→
    58→  return res.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
    59→}
    60→
    61→let cachedImage: { date: string; apod: ApodResponse } | null = null;
    62→
    63→async function findLatestImage(apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    64→  const today = formatDate(new Date());
    65→
    66→  if (cachedImage && cachedImage.date === today) {
    67→    return cachedImage.apod;
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  let videoStreak = 0;
    71→
    72→  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
    73→    const d = new Date();
    74→    d.setUTCDate(d.getUTCDate() - i);
    75→
    76→    const apod = await fetchApod(formatDate(d), apiKey);
    77→    if (apod.media_type === "image") {
    78→      cachedImage = { date: today, apod };
    79→      return apod;
    80→    }
    81→    videoStreak++;
    82→  }
    83→
    84→  throw new Error(
    85→    `Today's APOD is a video, and the last ${videoStreak} days have been too — no image available`
    86→  );
    87→}
    88→
    89→async function fetchImageCached(url: string): Promise<Response> {
    90→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    91→    cf: { cacheTtl: CACHE_TTL, cacheEverything: true },
    92→  });
    93→
    94→  if (!res.ok) {
    95→    throw new Error(`Image fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
    96→  }
    97→
    98→  return res;
    99→}
   100→
   101→function baseHeaders(origin: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
   102→  return {
   103→    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   104→    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
   105→    "Link": [
   106→      `<${origin}/>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="HD image"`,
   107→      `<${origin}/sd>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="SD image"`,
   108→      `<${origin}/info>; rel="describedby"`,
   109→      `<${origin}/about>; rel="about"`,
   110→    ].join(", "),
   111→    ...headers,
   112→  };
   113→}
   114→
   115→export default {
   116→  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
   117→    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
   118→      return new Response(null, {
   119→        headers: {
   120→          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   121→          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, OPTIONS",
   122→          "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
   123→        },
   124→      });
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    const reqUrl = new URL(request.url);
   128→    const { pathname } = reqUrl;
   129→    const origin = reqUrl.origin;
   130→
   131→    try {
   132→      const apiKey = env.NASA_API_KEY || "DEMO_KEY";
   133→
   134→      if (pathname === "/" || pathname === "/sd") {
   135→        const apod = await findLatestImage(apiKey);
   136→        const imageUrl = pathname === "/sd"
   137→          ? apod.url
   138→          : (apod.hdurl || apod.url);
   139→
   140→        const upstream = await fetchImageCached(imageUrl);
   141→        const contentType = upstream.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/jpeg";
   142→
   143→        return new Response(upstream.body, {
   144→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),
   145→        });
   146→      }
   147→
   148→      if (pathname === "/info") {
   149→        const endpoints = {
   150→          "/": "Today's APOD image (high resolution, falls back to standard)",
   151→          "/sd": "Today's APOD image (standard resolution)",
   152→          "/info": "JSON metadata for today's APOD",
   153→          "/about": "Plain-text description and attribution",
   154→        };
   155→
   156→        try {
   157→          const apod = await fetchApod(formatDate(new Date()), apiKey);
   158→
   159→          return new Response(
   160→            JSON.stringify({
   161→              title: apod.title,
   162→              date: apod.date,
   163→              explanation: apod.explanation,
   164→              media_type: apod.media_type,
   165→              url: apod.url,
   166→              hdurl: apod.hdurl ?? null,
   167→              copyright: apod.copyright ?? null,
   168→              endpoints,
   169→            }, null, 2),
   170→            { headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   171→          );
   172→        } catch (err) {
   173→          const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   174→          const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   175→          return new Response(
   176→            JSON.stringify({ error: message, endpoints }, null, 2),
   177→            { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   178→          );
   179→        }
   180→      }
   181→
   182→      if (pathname === "/about") {
   183→        const text = [
   184→          "",
   185→          "     .        *          .          *          .        *",
   186→          "   .         *                        .          .",
   187→          "                 _   _    _    ____    _",
   188→          "     *          | \\ | |  / \\  / ___|  / \\         .",
   189→          "        .       |  \\| | / _ \\ \\___ \\ / _ \\",
   190→          "           .    | |\\  |/ ___ \\ ___) / ___ \\   *",
   191→          "     .          |_| \\_/_/   \\_\\____/_/   \\_\\        .",
   192→          "  *                              .                *",
   193→          "        .    *        .    *          .        .",
   194→          "",
   195→          "                   ── APOD Proxy ──",
   196→          "",
   197→          "  Proxy for NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day.",
   198→          "  Returns today's image at a static URL, no API key required.",
   199→          "  Video days are skipped automatically.",
   200→          "  Useful for wallpapers, <img> sources, and other zero-JS environments.",
   201→          "",
   202→          "  Usage:",
   203→          `    <img src="${origin}/">`,
   204→          "",
   205→          "  Routes:",
   206→          "    /        HD image (default)",
   207→          "    /sd      Standard resolution",
   208→          "    /info    JSON metadata",
   209→          "    /about   This page",
   210→          "",
   211→          "  Built by Grant Keller — https://grantkeller.dev",
   212→          "",
   213→        ].join("\n");
   214→
   215→        return new Response(text, {
   216→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" }),
   217→        });
   218→      }
   219→
   220→      return new Response(
   221→        JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found. Routes: /, /sd, /info, /about" }),
   222→        { status: 404, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   223→      );
   224→    } catch (err) {
   225→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   226→      const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   227→      return new Response(
   228→        JSON.stringify({ error: message }),
   229→        { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   230→      );
   231→    }
   232→  },
   233→};
   234→
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→---
     2→name: Domain strategy
     3→description: Planned domain structure for nasapicture.com — landing page, API subdomain, and human-friendly subdomain
     4→type: project
     5→---
     6→
     7→Domain: nasapicture.com (available, not yet purchased)
     8→
     9→- `nasapicture.com` → static splash/landing page (Cloudflare Pages)
    10→- `api.nasapicture.com` → the Worker (developer-facing)
    11→- `get.nasapicture.com` → same Worker (human-friendly, for sharing)
    12→
    13→Both subdomains are CNAME records to the same Worker. Temp testing domain: nasapic.grantkeller.dev
    14→
    15→**Why:** The API should always return raw images at `/` — no content negotiation or HTML. Separate landing page lets the splash be as fancy as needed without complicating the Worker. `get.` subdomain is for non-technical people who'd stumble over `api.`.
    16→
    17→**How to apply:** When setting up DNS and deploying, configure both subdomains as custom domains on the same Worker. Landing page is a separate Cloudflare Pages project.
    18→

<teammate-message teammate_id="researcher" color="blue" summary="Landing page research brief completed">
## Landing Page Research Brief

**Task #1 Completed**: Research codebase context for landing page

### /about Endpoint Tone & ASCII Art

The Worker's `/about` endpoint has a distinctive engineering-forward personality with character. Here's the ASCII art header:

```
     .        *          .          *          .        *
   .         *                        .          .
                 _   _    _    ____    _
     *          | \ | |  / \  / ___|  / \         .
        .       |  \| | / _ \ \___ \ / _ \
           .    | |\  |/ ___ \ ___) / ___ \   *
     .          |_| \_/_/   \_\____/_/   \_\        .
  *                              .                *
        .    *        .    *          .        .

                   ── APOD Proxy ──
```

**Tone**: Space-themed ASCII with dotted stars, uses en-dashes (──), describes the proxy as practical and zero-friction ("no API key required", "zero-JS environments"). Mentions Grant Keller credit and his personal domain.

### Four Endpoints

From `/info` and `/about`:
1. **GET /** → "Today's APOD image (high resolution, falls back to standard)"
2. **GET /sd** → "Today's APOD image (standard resolution)"  
3. **GET /info** → "JSON metadata for today's APOD"
4. **GET /about** → "Plain-text description and attribution"

### Domain Structure

- **nasapicture.com** → landing page (Cloudflare Pages, this repo's `landing/` dir)
- **api.nasapicture.com** → Worker (developer-facing, proxies APOD)
- **get.nasapicture.com** → same Worker (human-friendly for sharing)

The landing page lives in the same repo as the Worker but deploys separately via Cloudflare Pages.

### Design Constraints (from CLAUDE.md)

✓ Dark theme (space photos fit the APOD aesthetic)
✓ Engineering-forward tone with personality (follow /about endpoint style)
✓ No generic fonts (avoid Inter, Roboto, system fonts)
✓ No AI slop — creative, distinctive design
✓ WCAG AA contrast ratios
✓ Content needed:
  - Hero: APOD image via `<img src="https://api.nasapicture.com/">`
  - Explanation: What the proxy is, why it exists
  - Endpoint listing: All four routes with descriptions
  - Credit: Grant Keller link to https://grantkeller.dev

### Implementation Plan

Single `index.html` in `/landing/` directory — no build step needed. Will reference the API endpoint for the live hero image. Should match the personality of `/about` but in HTML form (creative typography, space/astronomy aesthetic, clear endpoint documentation).

Ready for design and implementation phases.
</teammate-message>

      pointer-events: none;
    }

    .starfield::before,
    .starfield::after {
      content: '';

      inset: 0;
    }

    .starfield::before {
      background-image:
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 10% 20%, rgba(255,255,255,0.8), transparent),

        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 60% 15%, rgba(255,255,255,0.7), transparent),

        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 65% 95%, rgba(255,255,255,0.5), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 80% 45%, rgba(255,255,255,0.7), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1.5px 1.5px at 12% 72%, rgba(255,255,255,0.4), transparent);
      animation: twinkle 8s ease-in-out infinite alternate;
    }

    .starfield::after {
      background-image:
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 8% 35%, rgba(255,255,255,0.5), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1.5px 1.5px at 22% 78%, rgba(255,255,255,0.7), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 38% 52%, rgba(255,255,255,0.4), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 52% 18%, rgba(255,255,255,0.8), transparent),

        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 82% 88%, rgba(255,255,255,0.5), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 92% 25%, rgba(255,255,255,0.7), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1.5px 1.5px at 18% 62%, rgba(255,255,255,0.3), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 42% 8%, rgba(255,255,255,0.6), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 58% 75%, rgba(255,255,255,0.4), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1.5px 1.5px at 72% 15%, rgba(255,255,255,0.8), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 88% 58%, rgba(255,255,255,0.5), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 3% 92%, rgba(255,255,255,0.6), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1.5px 1.5px at 48% 32%, rgba(255,255,255,0.7), transparent),
        radial-gradient(1px 1px at 78% 72%, rgba(255,255,255,0.4), transparent);
      animation: twinkle 12s ease-in-out infinite alternate-reverse;
    }

    @keyframes twinkle {
      0% { opacity: 0.5; }
      50% { opacity: 1; }
      100% { opacity: 0.6; }
    }

    /* Staggered fade-in */
    .fade-in {
      opacity: 0;
      transform: translateY(20px);
      animation: fadeUp 0.8s ease-out forwards;
    }

    .fade-in-delay-1 { animation-delay: 0.15s; }
    .fade-in-delay-2 { animation-delay: 0.3s; }
    .fade-in-delay-3 { animation-delay: 0.5s; }
    .fade-in-delay-4 { animation-delay: 0.7s; }

    @keyframes fadeUp {
      to {
        opacity: 1;
        transform: translateY(0);
      }
    }

    /* Hero image glow */
    .hero-glow {
      position: absolute;
      inset: -40px;
      background: radial-gradient(ellipse at center, rgba(232, 168, 56, 0.12) 0%, transparent 70%);
      z-index: -1;
      border-radius: 50%;
      filter: blur(40px);
    }

    /* Endpoint card hover */
    .endpoint-card {
      transition: border-color 0.3s ease, box-shadow 0.3s ease, transform 0.2s ease;
    }

    .endpoint-card:hover {
      border-color: rgba(232, 168, 56, 0.4);
      box-shadow: 0 0 24px rgba(232, 168, 56, 0.08);
      transform: translateY(-2px);
    }

    /* Reduced motion */
    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
      .starfield::before,
      .starfield::after {
        animation: none;
        opacity: 0.7;
      }

      .fade-in {
        animation: none;
        opacity: 1;
        transform: none;
      }

      .endpoint-card {
        transition: none;
      }

      .endpoint-card:hover {
        transform: none;
      }
    }

    /* Image loading skeleton */
    .img-skeleton {
      background: linear-gradient(110deg, #111827 8%, #1a2332 18%, #111827 33%);
      background-size: 200% 100%;
      animation: shimmer 1.5s linear infinite;
    }

    @keyframes shimmer {
      to { background-position: -200% 0; }
    }

    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
      .img-skeleton {
        animation: none;
        background: #111827;
      }
    }
  </style>
</head>

<body class="bg-void text-silver font-sans min-h-screen relative">
  <div class="starfield" aria-hidden="true"></div>

  <div class="relative z-10">
    <!-- Header -->
    <header class="fade-in px-6 py-6 sm:px-8 max-w-5xl mx-auto flex items-baseline justify-between">
      <a href="/" class="group flex items-baseline gap-2">
        <span class="font-serif text-2xl sm:text-3xl text-white tracking-tight">nasapicture</span>
        <span class="font-serif text-2xl sm:text-3xl text-starlight">.com</span>
      </a>
      <nav>
        <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/about"
           class="font-mono text-sm text-silver-dim hover:text-starlight transition-colors"
           target="_blank"
           rel="noopener noreferrer">/about</a>
      </nav>
    </header>

    <main class="px-6 sm:px-8 max-w-5xl mx-auto pb-20">
      <!-- Hero -->
      <section class="fade-in fade-in-delay-1 mb-16 sm:mb-24">
        <div class="relative max-w-3xl mx-auto">
          <div class="hero-glow" aria-hidden="true"></div>
          <div class="relative rounded-lg overflow-hidden img-skeleton aspect-video">
            <img
              src="https://api.nasapicture.com/"
              alt="NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day"
              class="w-full h-full object-cover"
              loading="eager"
              onload="this.parentElement.classList.remove('img-skeleton')"
            >
          </div>
          <p class="mt-4 text-center font-serif italic text-lg sm:text-xl text-silver-dim">
            Today's Astronomy Picture of the Day
          </p>
        </div>
      </section>

      <!-- Explanation -->
      <section class="fade-in fade-in-delay-2 mb-16 sm:mb-24 max-w-2xl mx-auto">
        <h2 class="font-serif text-3xl sm:text-4xl text-white mb-6">
          One URL. Today's picture.
        </h2>
        <p class="text-lg leading-relaxed mb-4">
          <span class="text-starlight font-medium">nasapicture.com</span> is a proxy for
          NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day. It gives you a stable URL that always
          returns today's APOD image &mdash; no API key, no JavaScript, no configuration.
        </p>
        <p class="text-lg leading-relaxed mb-4">
          Drop it into an <code class="font-mono text-sm bg-void-light px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded border border-teal">&lt;img&gt;</code> tag,
          use it as a wallpaper source, or fetch the JSON metadata. Video days
          are skipped automatically &mdash; you always get an image.
        </p>
        <div class="mt-8 bg-void-light border border-teal rounded-lg p-5">
          <p class="font-mono text-sm text-silver-dim mb-2">// That's it. That's the whole integration.</p>
          <p class="font-mono text-sm sm:text-base text-white">
            &lt;img src="<span class="text-starlight">https://api.nasapicture.com/</span>"&gt;
          </p>
        </div>
      </section>

      <!-- Endpoints -->
      <section class="fade-in fade-in-delay-3 mb-16 sm:mb-24">
        <h2 class="font-serif text-3xl sm:text-4xl text-white mb-3 max-w-2xl mx-auto">Endpoints</h2>
        <p class="text-silver-dim mb-8 max-w-2xl mx-auto">
          All routes are on <code class="font-mono text-sm text-starlight-dim">api.nasapicture.com</code>
        </p>

        <div class="grid gap-4 sm:grid-cols-2 max-w-2xl mx-auto">
          <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/"
             target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"
             class="endpoint-card block bg-void-light border border-teal rounded-lg p-5">
            <div class="flex items-center gap-3 mb-2">
              <span class="font-mono text-base font-medium text-starlight">/</span>
              <span class="text-xs font-mono text-silver-dim uppercase tracking-wider">get</span>
            </div>
            <p class="text-sm text-silver leading-relaxed">
              Today's APOD in high resolution. Falls back to standard if HD isn't available.
            </p>
          </a>

          <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/sd"
             target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"
             class="endpoint-card block bg-void-light border border-teal rounded-lg p-5">
            <div class="flex items-center gap-3 mb-2">
              <span class="font-mono text-base font-medium text-starlight">/sd</span>
              <span class="text-xs font-mono text-silver-dim uppercase tracking-wider">get</span>
            </div>
            <p class="text-sm text-silver leading-relaxed">
              Standard resolution image. Lighter payload, same picture.
            </p>
          </a>

          <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/info"
             target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"
             class="endpoint-card block bg-void-light border border-teal rounded-lg p-5">
            <div class="flex items-center gap-3 mb-2">
              <span class="font-mono text-base font-medium text-starlight">/info</span>
              <span class="text-xs font-mono text-silver-dim uppercase tracking-wider">get</span>
            </div>
            <p class="text-sm text-silver leading-relaxed">
              JSON metadata &mdash; title, date, explanation, image URLs, copyright.
            </p>
          </a>

          <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/about"
             target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"
             class="endpoint-card block bg-void-light border border-teal rounded-lg p-5">
            <div class="flex items-center gap-3 mb-2">
              <span class="font-mono text-base font-medium text-starlight">/about</span>
              <span class="text-xs font-mono text-silver-dim uppercase tracking-wider">get</span>
            </div>
            <p class="text-sm text-silver leading-relaxed">
              Plain-text description with ASCII art. Good for terminals.
            </p>
          </a>
        </div>
      </section>

      <!-- How it works -->
      <section class="fade-in fade-in-delay-4 max-w-2xl mx-auto">
        <h2 class="font-serif text-3xl sm:text-4xl text-white mb-6">How it works</h2>
        <div class="space-y-4 text-lg leading-relaxed">
          <p>
            A <a href="https://workers.cloudflare.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-starlight hover:underline underline-offset-4">Cloudflare Worker</a>
            fetches today's APOD from NASA's API, then streams the image bytes
            directly to you. The response is cached at the edge &mdash; subsequent
            requests in the same hour are served instantly from Cloudflare's network.
          </p>
          <p>
            If today's APOD is a video (it happens), the worker walks backwards up to
            7 days to find the most recent image. You never get an error for something
            out of your control.
          </p>
        </div>
      </section>
    </main>

    <!-- Footer -->
    <footer class="relative z-10 border-t border-teal px-6 sm:px-8 py-8">
      <div class="max-w-5xl mx-auto flex flex-col sm:flex-row items-center justify-between gap-4 text-sm text-silver-dim">
        <p>
          Built by
          <a href="https://grantkeller.dev" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"
             class="text-silver hover:text-starlight transition-colors">Grant Keller</a>
        </p>
        <p>
          Data from
          <a href="https://apod.nasa.gov/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"
             class="text-silver hover:text-starlight transition-colors">NASA APOD</a>
        </p>
      </div>
    </footer>
  </div>
</body>
</html>

<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Research brief and unblock for design/build">
Task #1 (research) is complete. Here's the brief — you're unblocked to start Task #2 (design) and then Task #3 (build).

**Tone reference** — the /about endpoint ASCII art:
```
     .        *          .          *          .        *
   .         *                        .          .
                 _   _    _    ____    _
     *          | \ | |  / \  / ___|  / \         .
        .       |  \| | / _ \ \___ \ / _ \
           .    | |\  |/ ___ \ ___) / ___ \   *
     .          |_| \_/_/   \_\____/_/   \_\        .
  *                              .                *
        .    *        .    *          .        .
                   ── APOD Proxy ──
```

**Four endpoints** (all on api.nasapicture.com):
1. GET / → HD APOD image (falls back to standard)
2. GET /sd → Standard resolution
3. GET /info → JSON metadata
4. GET /about → Plain-text description

**Domain structure**:
- nasapicture.com → this landing page
- api.nasapicture.com → Worker API
- get.nasapicture.com → same Worker, human-friendly for sharing

**Design constraints**:
- Dark space theme, atmospheric background (not flat dark)
- No generic fonts — choose distinctive typography
- Engineering-forward tone with personality
- WCAG AA, semantic HTML, prefers-reduced-motion
- Creative and distinctive, no AI slop

**Content**:
- Hero: `<img src="https://api.nasapicture.com/" alt="NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day">`
- Explanation of the proxy (practical, zero-friction, no API key needed)
- Endpoint listing with all four routes
- Credit: "Built by Grant Keller" → https://grantkeller.dev

Go ahead and start on Task #2 (design), then #3 (build). Create the file at landing/index.html.
</teammate-message>

_**User (2026-03-26 08:03:33Z)**_

<teammate-message teammate_id="builder" color="green" summary="Landing page build complete">
Tasks #2 (design) and #3 (build) are complete. The landing page is at `/Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/landing/index.html`.

**Design choices:**
- **Fonts**: Instrument Serif (headings), DM Sans (body), JetBrains Mono (code) — all via Google Fonts
- **Colors**: Deep navy (#0a0e17) background, warm amber/gold (#e8a838) accent (deliberate contrast to typical blue/purple space sites), silver text, teal card borders
- **Background**: CSS-only starfield with two layers of radial gradients that twinkle at different rates, plus a warm glow behind the hero image

**What's in the page:**
- Hero section with the live APOD image from `https://api.nasapicture.com/`
- "One URL. Today's picture." explanation section with inline code example
- 4 endpoint cards in a 2x2 grid (/, /sd, /info, /about) — all linking to api.nasapicture.com
- "How it works" section explaining the Cloudflare Worker + edge caching + video fallback
- Footer crediting Grant Keller with link to grantkeller.dev, plus NASA APOD attribution

**Accessibility & UX:**
- Semantic HTML (header, main, nav, section, footer)
- WCAG AA contrast maintained throughout
- `prefers-reduced-motion` disables all animations (twinkle, fade-in, hover transforms)
- Loading skeleton with shimmer for the hero image while it loads
- All external links have `rel="noopener noreferrer"` and `target="_blank"`
- Mobile-responsive with Tailwind's responsive classes
- Staggered fade-in animations on page load for visual delight

**Single self-contained file** — Tailwind via CDN, Google Fonts via CDN, no build step needed.
</teammate-message>

Base directory for this skill: /Users/grant/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/impeccable/.claude/skills/teach-impeccable

Gather design context for this project, then persist it for all future sessions.

## Step 1: Explore the Codebase

Before asking questions, thoroughly scan the project to discover what you can:

- **README and docs**: Project purpose, target audience, any stated goals
- **Package.json / config files**: Tech stack, dependencies, existing design libraries
- **Existing components**: Current design patterns, spacing, typography in use
- **Brand assets**: Logos, favicons, color values already defined
- **Design tokens / CSS variables**: Existing color palettes, font stacks, spacing scales
- **Any style guides or brand documentation**

Note what you've learned and what remains unclear.

## Step 2: Ask UX-Focused Questions

STOP and call the AskUserQuestionTool to clarify. Focus only on what you couldn't infer from the codebase:

### Users & Purpose
- Who uses this? What's their context when using it?
- What job are they trying to get done?
- What emotions should the interface evoke? (confidence, delight, calm, urgency, etc.)

### Brand & Personality
- How would you describe the brand personality in 3 words?
- Any reference sites or apps that capture the right feel? What specifically about them?
- What should this explicitly NOT look like? Any anti-references?

### Aesthetic Preferences
- Any strong preferences for visual direction? (minimal, bold, elegant, playful, technical, organic, etc.)
- Light mode, dark mode, or both?
- Any colors that must be used or avoided?

### Accessibility & Inclusion
- Specific accessibility requirements? (WCAG level, known user needs)
- Considerations for reduced motion, color blindness, or other accommodations?

Skip questions where the answer is already clear from the codebase exploration.

## Step 3: Write Design Context

Synthesize your findings and the user's answers into a `## Design Context` section:

```markdown
## Design Context

### Users
[Who they are, their context, the job to be done]

### Brand Personality
[Voice, tone, 3-word personality, emotional goals]

### Aesthetic Direction
[Visual tone, references, anti-references, theme]

### Design Principles
[3-5 principles derived from the conversation that should guide all design decisions]
```

Write this section to CLAUDE.md in the project root. If the file exists, append or update the Design Context section.

Confirm completion and summarize the key design principles that will now guide all future work.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 08:37:00Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./README.md`

```text
     1→# NASA Picture Proxy
     2→
     3→A Cloudflare Worker that proxies [NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day](https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/astropix.html) behind a fixed URL. An HTTP GET request returns today's image without needing an API key or parsing JSON.
     4→
     5→During a tinkering session, I wanted to set the background image of my homelab [homepage](https://github.com/gethomepage/homepage) dashboard to the APOD. I didn't want to inject additional JavaScript on the page and couldn't find an existing static URL. So, I built this proxy to share with anyone else who'd like to view the wonders of space easily, anywhere.
     6→
     7→
     8→## Usage
     9→
    10→Embed directly as an image:
    11→
    12→```html
    13→<img src="https://api.nasapicture.com/" alt="NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day" />
    14→```
    15→
    16→CSS background:
    17→
    18→```css
    19→body {
    20→  background-image: url("https://api.nasapicture.com/");
    21→  background-size: cover;
    22→}
    23→```
    24→
    25→Fetch metadata:
    26→
    27→```bash
    28→curl https://api.nasapicture.com/info | jq .
    29→```
    30→
    31→## Endpoints
    32→
    33→| Path | Response |
    34→|------|----------|
    35→| `/` | Today's APOD image (HD, falls back to standard) |
    36→| `/sd` | Standard resolution image |
    37→| `/info` | JSON metadata (title, date, explanation, copyright) |
    38→| `/about` | Plain-text description and route listing |
    39→
    40→When the APOD of the day is a video, the proxy walks backwards up to 7 days to find the most recent image.
    41→
    42→## How it works
    43→
    44→Both the NASA API call and the upstream image fetch are cached at Cloudflare's edge via `cf: { cacheTtl, cacheEverything }`. Caching is purely edge-based; there's no KV, D1, or cron involved.
    45→
    46→When the date rolls over, `formatDate(new Date())` produces a new date string, which changes the API request URL and therefore the cache key. The response updates automatically.
    47→
    48→## Author
    49→
    50→Built by [Grant Keller](https://grantkeller.dev).
    51→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./CLAUDE.md`

```text
     1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## Project Overview
     6→
     7→APOD Proxy — a Cloudflare Worker that proxies NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day API behind stable, fixed URLs. Serves today's APOD image (standard or HD) or JSON metadata. Falls back up to 7 days when the daily APOD is a video.
     8→
     9→## Commands
    10→
    11→```bash
    12→npm run dev        # Local dev server at http://localhost:8787
    13→npm run deploy     # Deploy to Cloudflare Workers
    14→npx tsc --noEmit   # Type-check (no build artifact — Wrangler bundles directly)
    15→```
    16→
    17→## Architecture
    18→
    19→Single-file Worker (`src/index.ts`) with four routes:
    20→
    21→- `GET /` — proxies today's HD APOD image (falls back to standard if unavailable)
    22→- `GET /sd` — proxies the standard-res version
    23→- `GET /info` — returns JSON metadata
    24→- `GET /about` — plain-text description and attribution
    25→
    26→Caching is handled entirely by Cloudflare's edge cache via `cf: { cacheTtl, cacheEverything }` on fetch calls — no KV, D1, or cron. Cache key changes naturally when the date rolls over (new date string = new API URL).
    27→
    28→The `findLatestImage` function walks backwards from today up to `MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS` (7) to skip video APODs.
    29→
    30→## Environment
    31→
    32→- **`NASA_API_KEY`** — optional secret; falls back to `DEMO_KEY` (30 req/hr, sufficient behind cache). Set via `npx wrangler secret put NASA_API_KEY`.
    33→- Local secrets go in `.dev.vars` (gitignored).
    34→
    35→## Project Board
    36→
    37→This project uses a structured board system for project management.
    38→
    39→- **Ask before creating new board files** — don't auto-create tasks, ADRs, etc.
    40→- Follow the board system spec for all documentation artifacts
    41→- Use `/board:board-show` to see current board state
    42→- Use `/board:board-add` to create new items
    43→- Use `/board:board-update` after completing significant work to reconcile the board
    44→- Use `/board:board-check` when starting work to find related board context
    45→
```
</details></tool-use>

Done. The **Design Context** section is now in `CLAUDE.md` and will guide all future design work. Here's the summary:

**Brand**: Clever, minimal, precise
**Emotion**: Awe + simplicity
**5 design principles**:
1. The image is the hero — never compete with the APOD
2. Earn trust through restraint — fewer elements, better
3. Craft in the details — personality in small moments, not loud choices
4. Accessible by default — not optional
5. One file, zero friction — mirrors the API philosophy

_**User (2026-03-26 08:46:07Z)**_

Base directory for this skill: /Users/grant/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/claude-plugins-official/plugins/frontend-design/skills/frontend-design

This skill guides creation of distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces that avoid generic "AI slop" aesthetics. Implement real working code with exceptional attention to aesthetic details and creative choices.

The user provides frontend requirements: a component, page, application, or interface to build. They may include context about the purpose, audience, or technical constraints.

## Design Thinking

Before coding, understand the context and commit to a BOLD aesthetic direction:
- **Purpose**: What problem does this interface solve? Who uses it?
- **Tone**: Pick an extreme: brutally minimal, maximalist chaos, retro-futuristic, organic/natural, luxury/refined, playful/toy-like, editorial/magazine, brutalist/raw, art deco/geometric, soft/pastel, industrial/utilitarian, etc. There are so many flavors to choose from. Use these for inspiration but design one that is true to the aesthetic direction.
- **Constraints**: Technical requirements (framework, performance, accessibility).
- **Differentiation**: What makes this UNFORGETTABLE? What's the one thing someone will remember?

**CRITICAL**: Choose a clear conceptual direction and execute it with precision. Bold maximalism and refined minimalism both work - the key is intentionality, not intensity.

Then implement working code (HTML/CSS/JS, React, Vue, etc.) that is:
- Production-grade and functional
- Visually striking and memorable
- Cohesive with a clear aesthetic point-of-view
- Meticulously refined in every detail

## Frontend Aesthetics Guidelines

Focus on:
- **Typography**: Choose fonts that are beautiful, unique, and interesting. Avoid generic fonts like Arial and Inter; opt instead for distinctive choices that elevate the frontend's aesthetics; unexpected, characterful font choices. Pair a distinctive display font with a refined body font.
- **Color & Theme**: Commit to a cohesive aesthetic. Use CSS variables for consistency. Dominant colors with sharp accents outperform timid, evenly-distributed palettes.
- **Motion**: Use animations for effects and micro-interactions. Prioritize CSS-only solutions for HTML. Use Motion library for React when available. Focus on high-impact moments: one well-orchestrated page load with staggered reveals (animation-delay) creates more delight than scattered micro-interactions. Use scroll-triggering and hover states that surprise.
- **Spatial Composition**: Unexpected layouts. Asymmetry. Overlap. Diagonal flow. Grid-breaking elements. Generous negative space OR controlled density.
- **Backgrounds & Visual Details**: Create atmosphere and depth rather than defaulting to solid colors. Add contextual effects and textures that match the overall aesthetic. Apply creative forms like gradient meshes, noise textures, geometric patterns, layered transparencies, dramatic shadows, decorative borders, custom cursors, and grain overlays.

NEVER use generic AI-generated aesthetics like overused font families (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system fonts), cliched color schemes (particularly purple gradients on white backgrounds), predictable layouts and component patterns, and cookie-cutter design that lacks context-specific character.

Interpret creatively and make unexpected choices that feel genuinely designed for the context. No design should be the same. Vary between light and dark themes, different fonts, different aesthetics. NEVER converge on common choices (Space Grotesk, for example) across generations.

**IMPORTANT**: Match implementation complexity to the aesthetic vision. Maximalist designs need elaborate code with extensive animations and effects. Minimalist or refined designs need restraint, precision, and careful attention to spacing, typography, and subtle details. Elegance comes from executing the vision well.

Remember: Claude is capable of extraordinary creative work. Don't hold back, show what can truly be created when thinking outside the box and committing fully to a distinctive vision.

The current landing page is solid but conventional. Let me reimagine it around the core principle: **the image is the hero**.

**Concept**: The APOD image fills the entire viewport like a spacecraft window. UI elements float over it as a minimal heads-up display. Scrolling descends from the cosmos into a dark "ground control" section with technical details. The memorable moment: opening the page feels like looking out into space.

  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Cormorant+Garamond:ital,wght@0,300;0,400;0,600;1,300;1,400&family=Outfit:wght@300;400;500&family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
  <style>
    :root {
      --void: #06080d;
      --void-up: #0c1018;
      --amber: #d4a04a;
      --amber-dim: #8b6a30;
      --amber-glow: rgba(212, 160, 74, 0.08);
      --silver: #b8bcc6;
      --silver-dim: #5a5f6b;
      --white: #e8eaed;
      --border: rgba(212, 160, 74, 0.12);
      --border-hover: rgba(212, 160, 74, 0.3);
      --font-serif: 'Cormorant Garamond', Georgia, serif;
      --font-sans: 'Outfit', sans-serif;
      --font-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
    }

    *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }

    html { scroll-behavior: smooth; }

    body {
      font-family: var(--font-sans);
      font-weight: 300;
      color: var(--silver);
      background: var(--void);
      -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
    }

    /* ── VIEWPORT HERO ── */
    .hero {
      position: relative;
      height: 100svh;
      min-height: 500px;
      overflow: hidden;
      display: flex;
      flex-direction: column;
    }

    .hero-image-wrap {
      position: absolute;
      inset: 0;
      z-index: 0;
    }

    .hero-image-wrap::after {
      content: '';
      position: absolute;
      inset: 0;
      background:
        linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(6,8,13,0.15) 0%, rgba(6,8,13,0) 30%, rgba(6,8,13,0) 50%, rgba(6,8,13,0.7) 85%, rgba(6,8,13,1) 100%),
        linear-gradient(to right, rgba(6,8,13,0.3) 0%, rgba(6,8,13,0) 20%, rgba(6,8,13,0) 80%, rgba(6,8,13,0.3) 100%);
      z-index: 1;
    }

    .hero-img {
      width: 100%;
      height: 100%;
      object-fit: cover;
      opacity: 0;
      transition: opacity 1.2s ease;
    }

    .hero-img.loaded { opacity: 1; }

    .hero-skeleton {
      position: absolute;
      inset: 0;
      background: linear-gradient(135deg, #0c1018 0%, #111620 50%, #0c1018 100%);
      background-size: 400% 400%;
      animation: skeleton-drift 3s ease-in-out infinite;
    }

    .hero-skeleton.hidden { display: none; }

    @keyframes skeleton-drift {
      0%, 100% { background-position: 0% 50%; }
      50% { background-position: 100% 50%; }
    }

    /* hero header */
    .hero-header {
      position: relative;
      z-index: 2;
      display: flex;
      justify-content: space-between;
      align-items: baseline;
      padding: 1.5rem 2rem;
    }

    .logo {
      font-family: var(--font-serif);
      font-size: 1.5rem;
      font-weight: 300;
      letter-spacing: -0.02em;
      color: var(--white);
      text-decoration: none;
      opacity: 0;
      animation: fade-in 1s ease 0.3s forwards;
    }

    .logo span { color: var(--amber); }

    .hero-nav {
      font-family: var(--font-mono);
      font-size: 0.75rem;
      opacity: 0;
      animation: fade-in 1s ease 0.5s forwards;
    }

    .hero-nav a {
      color: var(--silver-dim);
      text-decoration: none;
      transition: color 0.3s;
    }

    .hero-nav a:hover { color: var(--amber); }

    /* hero bottom overlay */
    .hero-bottom {
      position: relative;
      z-index: 2;
      margin-top: auto;
      padding: 0 2rem 3rem;
      max-width: 42rem;
    }

    .hero-title {
      font-family: var(--font-serif);
      font-size: clamp(2.5rem, 6vw, 4.5rem);
      font-weight: 300;
      line-height: 1.05;
      color: var(--white);
      letter-spacing: -0.03em;
      opacity: 0;
      animation: fade-up 1s ease 0.6s forwards;
    }

    .hero-subtitle {
      margin-top: 1rem;
      font-size: 1.05rem;
      line-height: 1.6;
      color: var(--silver);
      max-width: 32rem;
      opacity: 0;
      animation: fade-up 1s ease 0.8s forwards;
    }

    .scroll-hint {
      position: absolute;
      bottom: 1.5rem;
      right: 2rem;
      z-index: 2;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: 0.5rem;
      font-family: var(--font-mono);
      font-size: 0.65rem;
      letter-spacing: 0.1em;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      color: var(--silver-dim);
      opacity: 0;
      animation: fade-in 1s ease 1.2s forwards;
    }

    .scroll-line {
      width: 1px;
      height: 24px;
      background: var(--silver-dim);
      position: relative;
      overflow: hidden;
    }

    .scroll-line::after {
      content: '';
      position: absolute;
      top: -100%;
      left: 0;
      width: 100%;
      height: 100%;
      background: var(--amber);
      animation: scroll-pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite;
    }

    @keyframes scroll-pulse {
      0% { top: -100%; }
      50% { top: 100%; }
      100% { top: 100%; }
    }

    /* ── CONTENT SECTIONS ── */
    .content {
      position: relative;
      z-index: 1;
    }

    section {
      padding: 5rem 2rem;
    }

    .section-inner {
      max-width: 38rem;
      margin: 0 auto;
    }

    .section-wide {
      max-width: 48rem;
      margin: 0 auto;
    }

    h2 {
      font-family: var(--font-serif);
      font-size: clamp(2rem, 4vw, 3rem);
      font-weight: 300;
      color: var(--white);
      letter-spacing: -0.02em;
      line-height: 1.15;
      margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
    }

    p { line-height: 1.7; }

    p + p { margin-top: 1rem; }

    a.inline {
      color: var(--amber);
      text-decoration: none;
      text-underline-offset: 3px;
    }

    a.inline:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

    /* code snippet block */
    .code-block {
      margin-top: 2rem;
      background: var(--void-up);
      border: 1px solid var(--border);
      border-radius: 8px;
      padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem;
      font-family: var(--font-mono);
      font-size: 0.85rem;
      line-height: 1.7;
      overflow-x: auto;
    }

    .code-comment { color: var(--silver-dim); }
    .code-string { color: var(--amber); }
    .code-tag { color: var(--white); }

    /* ── DIVIDER ── */
    .divider {
      max-width: 38rem;
      margin: 0 auto;
      height: 1px;
      background: linear-gradient(to right, transparent, var(--border), transparent);
    }

    /* ── ENDPOINT GRID ── */
    .endpoint-grid {
      display: grid;
      gap: 1px;
      background: var(--border);
      border: 1px solid var(--border);
      border-radius: 8px;
      overflow: hidden;
      margin-top: 2rem;
    }

    @media (min-width: 640px) {
      .endpoint-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; }
    }

    .endpoint {
      display: block;
      background: var(--void-up);
      padding: 1.5rem;
      text-decoration: none;
      color: inherit;
      transition: background 0.3s, box-shadow 0.3s;
    }

    .endpoint:hover {
      background: #0f1420;
      box-shadow: inset 0 0 30px var(--amber-glow);
    }

    .endpoint-route {
      font-family: var(--font-mono);
      font-size: 0.9rem;
      font-weight: 500;
      color: var(--amber);
    }

    .endpoint-method {
      font-family: var(--font-mono);
      font-size: 0.6rem;
      letter-spacing: 0.12em;
      text-transform: uppercase;
      color: var(--silver-dim);
      margin-left: 0.75rem;
    }

    .endpoint-desc {
      margin-top: 0.5rem;
      font-size: 0.875rem;
      color: var(--silver);
      line-height: 1.5;
    }

    /* ── HOW IT WORKS ── */
    .mechanism {
      display: flex;
      gap: 1.25rem;
      align-items: flex-start;
    }

    .mechanism + .mechanism { margin-top: 1.75rem; }

    .mechanism-icon {
      flex-shrink: 0;
      width: 2rem;
      height: 2rem;
      border-radius: 50%;
      border: 1px solid var(--border);
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: center;
      font-family: var(--font-mono);
      font-size: 0.7rem;
      color: var(--amber-dim);
      margin-top: 0.15rem;
    }

    .mechanism p {
      font-size: 0.95rem;
    }

    /* ── FOOTER ── */
    footer {
      border-top: 1px solid var(--border);
      padding: 2rem;
    }

    .footer-inner {
      max-width: 48rem;
      margin: 0 auto;
      display: flex;
      justify-content: space-between;
      align-items: center;
      flex-wrap: wrap;
      gap: 1rem;
      font-size: 0.8rem;
      color: var(--silver-dim);
    }

    footer a {
      color: var(--silver);
      text-decoration: none;
      transition: color 0.3s;
    }

    footer a:hover { color: var(--amber); }

    /* ── ANIMATIONS ── */
    @keyframes fade-in {
      to { opacity: 1; }
    }

    @keyframes fade-up {
      to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
    }

    .fade-up {
      opacity: 0;
      transform: translateY(16px);
    }

    .fade-up.visible {
      animation: fade-up 0.7s ease forwards;
    }

    /* stagger children */
    .stagger-1 { animation-delay: 0.1s; }
    .stagger-2 { animation-delay: 0.2s; }

    /* ── REDUCED MOTION ── */
    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
      *, *::before, *::after {
        animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
        animation-delay: 0ms !important;
        transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
      }

      .hero-img { opacity: 1; }
      .logo, .hero-nav, .hero-title, .hero-subtitle,
      .scroll-hint, .fade-up { opacity: 1; transform: none; }

      .hero-skeleton { animation: none; }
    }

    /* ── RESPONSIVE ── */
    @media (max-width: 639px) {
      .hero-header { padding: 1.25rem 1.25rem; }
      .hero-bottom { padding: 0 1.25rem 2.5rem; }
      section { padding: 3.5rem 1.25rem; }
      .scroll-hint { display: none; }
      .footer-inner { flex-direction: column; text-align: center; }
    }
  </style>
</head>

<body>
  <!-- ── HERO: The image IS the experience ── -->
  <section class="hero" aria-label="Today's Astronomy Picture of the Day">
    <div class="hero-image-wrap">
      <div class="hero-skeleton" id="hero-skeleton" aria-hidden="true"></div>
      <img
        src="https://api.nasapicture.com/"
        alt="NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day"
        class="hero-img"
        id="hero-img"
        loading="eager"
        fetchpriority="high"
      >
    </div>

    <header class="hero-header">
      <a href="/" class="logo" aria-label="nasapicture.com home">
        nasapicture<span>.com</span>
      </a>
      <nav class="hero-nav" aria-label="Site navigation">
        <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/about" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">/about</a>
      </nav>
    </header>

    <div class="hero-bottom">
      <h1 class="hero-title">Today's picture<br>of the cosmos.</h1>
      <p class="hero-subtitle">
        A stable URL that always returns NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day.
        No API key. No JavaScript. Just an image tag.
      </p>
    </div>

    <div class="scroll-hint" aria-hidden="true">
      <div class="scroll-line"></div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- ── CONTENT ── -->
  <div class="content">

    <!-- Usage -->
    <section>
      <div class="section-inner">
        <h2 class="fade-up">One line of HTML.</h2>
        <p class="fade-up stagger-1">
          <strong style="color: var(--white); font-weight: 400;">nasapicture.com</strong> proxies
          NASA's APOD behind a fixed URL. Drop it into an
          <code style="font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 0.85em; color: var(--amber);">&lt;img&gt;</code>
          tag, set it as a wallpaper source, or fetch the JSON. Video days
          are skipped automatically &mdash; you always get an image.
        </p>
        <div class="code-block fade-up stagger-2">
          <div class="code-comment">// That's it. That's the whole integration.</div>
          <div><span class="code-tag">&lt;img</span> src="<span class="code-string">https://api.nasapicture.com/</span>"<span class="code-tag">&gt;</span></div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </section>

    <div class="divider"></div>

    <!-- Endpoints -->
    <section>
      <div class="section-wide">
        <div class="section-inner">
          <h2 class="fade-up">Endpoints</h2>
          <p class="fade-up stagger-1" style="color: var(--silver-dim);">
            All routes on
            <code style="font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 0.85em; color: var(--amber-dim);">api.nasapicture.com</code>
          </p>
        </div>

        <div class="endpoint-grid fade-up stagger-2">
          <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="endpoint">
            <div>
              <span class="endpoint-route">/</span>
              <span class="endpoint-method">get</span>
            </div>
            <p class="endpoint-desc">Today's APOD in high resolution. Falls back to standard if HD isn't available.</p>
          </a>
          <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/sd" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="endpoint">
            <div>
              <span class="endpoint-route">/sd</span>
              <span class="endpoint-method">get</span>
            </div>
            <p class="endpoint-desc">Standard resolution. Lighter payload, same picture.</p>
          </a>
          <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/info" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="endpoint">
            <div>
              <span class="endpoint-route">/info</span>
              <span class="endpoint-method">get</span>
            </div>
            <p class="endpoint-desc">JSON metadata &mdash; title, date, explanation, image URLs, copyright.</p>
          </a>
          <a href="https://api.nasapicture.com/about" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="endpoint">
            <div>
              <span class="endpoint-route">/about</span>
              <span class="endpoint-method">get</span>
            </div>
            <p class="endpoint-desc">Plain-text description with ASCII art. Good for terminals.</p>
          </a>
        </div>
      </div>
    </section>

    <div class="divider"></div>

    <!-- How it works -->
    <section>
      <div class="section-inner">
        <h2 class="fade-up">How it works</h2>

        <div class="fade-up stagger-1">
          <div class="mechanism">
            <div class="mechanism-icon" aria-hidden="true">1</div>
            <p>
              A <a href="https://workers.cloudflare.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="inline">Cloudflare Worker</a>
              fetches today's APOD from NASA's API and streams the image bytes directly to you.
            </p>
          </div>

          <div class="mechanism">
            <div class="mechanism-icon" aria-hidden="true">2</div>
            <p>
              The response is cached at the edge. Subsequent requests in the same hour are served
              instantly from Cloudflare's network &mdash; no origin roundtrip.
            </p>
          </div>

          <div class="mechanism">
            <div class="mechanism-icon" aria-hidden="true">3</div>
            <p>
              If today's APOD is a video, the worker walks backwards up to 7 days to find
              the most recent image. You never get an error for something out of your control.
            </p>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </section>

    <!-- Footer -->
    <footer>
      <div class="footer-inner">
        <p>Built by <a href="https://grantkeller.dev" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Grant Keller</a></p>
        <p>Data from <a href="https://apod.nasa.gov/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day</a></p>
      </div>
    </footer>
  </div>

  <script>
    // Image load handler
    const img = document.getElementById('hero-img');
    const skeleton = document.getElementById('hero-skeleton');

    function onImageReady() {
      img.classList.add('loaded');
      skeleton.classList.add('hidden');
    }

    if (img.complete && img.naturalWidth > 0) {
      onImageReady();
    } else {
      img.addEventListener('load', onImageReady);
      img.addEventListener('error', () => { skeleton.classList.add('hidden'); });
    }

    // Scroll-triggered fade-in
    const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
      entries.forEach(entry => {
        if (entry.isIntersecting) {
          entry.target.classList.add('visible');
          observer.unobserve(entry.target);
        }
      });
    }, { threshold: 0.15 });

    document.querySelectorAll('.fade-up').forEach(el => observer.observe(el));
  </script>
</body>
</html>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-26 08:48:38Z)**_

Opened in your browser. Here's what changed:

**The big move**: The APOD image now fills the entire viewport — edge to edge, 100svh. The UI floats over it. You're looking *through* a window into space, not at a card containing a picture.

**Key design choices**:
- **Cormorant Garamond** for headings — a high-contrast serif that reads like an observatory plaque, not a SaaS landing page
- **Outfit** for body — geometric, light (weight 300), disappears in the best way
- **No Tailwind CDN** — pure CSS, smaller payload, faster load, zero runtime
- **Viewport hero** with gradient vignette that fades the image into the void at bottom/edges
- **1px-gap endpoint grid** instead of cards with borders — cleaner, more intentional
- **Numbered mechanism steps** for "How it works" — scannable without being loud
- **Scroll-triggered fade-ups** via IntersectionObserver, plus a subtle animated scroll indicator
- **Skeleton** is a slow color drift instead of a shimmer stripe

Everything that was there before is still there — same content, same endpoints, same credits. Just recomposed so the cosmos comes first.
